page: UpCloud 24xCPU-96GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a 1920 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. There are no dedicated GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most standard workloads. The multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls within the middle 50% tier, though single-core stress-ng performance is in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached operations remains average. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression stands out as a strong point, ranking in the top 25%. However, text generation for medium-sized language models is poor, placing in the bottom 10%.

This server offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring significant parallel processing and local storage without the premium cost of dedicated CPU cores. The combination of 24 vCPUs and a 1920 GB SSD makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations. It is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded data processing, and medium-scale database hosting, while being less optimal for single-threaded tasks or intensive LLM text generation.
description: UpCloud 24xCPU-96GB is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 24 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz, 96.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 1920 GB local SSD. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most multi-core operations, database tasks, and memory bandwidth, though single-core performance is relatively weak. Notably, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs strongly, ranking in the top 25%. With its balanced 1:4 CPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local SSD storage, this server provides a cost-effective platform for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database hosting.
og_description: UpCloud 24xCPU-96GB is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 24 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB SSD, delivering balanced multi-core performance for databases and web serving.

tagline: A balanced multi-core virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and a high-capacity bundled SSD for general-purpose and database workloads.
bullet_points: 24 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 96.0 GB of system memory.
Bundled 1920 GB local SSD storage on a KVM hypervisor.
Strong multi-threaded decompression performance in the top 25%.
Balanced 1:4 CPU-to-RAM ratio suitable for general-purpose workloads.
Appropriate for software compilation, database hosting, and web serving.
